codeReview.summary: CONTRIBUTING.md was created correctly at repository root (248 lines) with all required content: PR workflow with branch naming convention, review requirements, and squash merge strategy; conventional commit conventions with all required prefixes (feat/fix/docs/refactor/test); and comprehensive code review process with reviewer checklist covering correctness, testing, code quality, integration, and security.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The docs-worker skill was referenced by the worker but the skill file does not exist in .factory/skills/docs-worker/. This is informational only - the worker completed the task correctly without the skill file, so the task was straightforward enough not to require it.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows worker loaded 'docs-worker' skill but cat .factory/skills/docs-worker/SKILL.md returned 'No such file or directory'

summary: The docs-contributing feature successfully created CONTRIBUTING.md at the repository root documenting: (1) PR workflow with branch naming (<type>/<short-description>), review requirements (1+ approval, CI pass), and squash merge strategy; (2) conventional commit conventions with feat/fix/docs/refactor/test/chore/perf/ci types and formatting rules; (3) code review process with checklists for authors and reviewers. The implementation fully satisfies VAL-DOC-011 requirements. Worker followed correct procedure and skillFeedback.confirmed procedure was followed.
